key_insight: Lagrangian Dual Flows add a dual co-state to flow matching so generated samples satisfy nonlinear constraints without projection or inner optimization loops.
engineering_relevance: This makes constrained generation practical for robotics, planning, and physics where outputs must obey complex inference-time constraints efficiently.
method: Jointly evolve the sample and a Lagrangian dual variable during denoising.
Use primal-dual dynamics to enforce nonlinear constraints directly.
Avoid expensive pseudoinverses, projections, and optimization subproblems at inference time.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a new constrained generation method with theoretical guarantees for nonlinear constraint satisfaction and claims it is simple and effective, but it does not provide specific benchmark results here.
watch_item: Performance will likely depend on the stability and tuning of the coupled primal-dual dynamics, especially for stiff or highly nonlinear constraints.
paper_title: Constrained Flow Matching via Lagrangian Dual Flows
